title: Hot, cold — and มาก
pedagogy: Vocabulary: ร้อน (LOW ร + mai tho → high), หนาว (ห-nam น: high + live → rising), มาก (low ม + dead-long → falling — the low-class long-dead rule in the wild). Pattern: adjective + มาก intensifies; weather talk is the universal Thai opener.

exercises.title: Weather talk is the national handshake
body: ร้อนมาก — “so hot” — may be the most-said sentence in Thailand. Agree with it and you've made a friend.
มาก is the amplifier, always after the word it boosts: ร้อนมาก, อร่อยมาก, ขอบคุณมาก.
Its tone is a textbook case: low-class ม + long vowel + stop ก = dead-long syllable = falling. The rare rule, in a word you'll say daily.

exercises.explanation: Adjective first, มาก after. Thai never says “very hot” — always “hot very”.
วันนี้ร้อนมาก — a four-word weather report with no verb “to be” anywhere. Thai adjectives do the being themselves.
